title: Do Asuka haters just see the worst part of themselves in her
display_title: Do Asuka haters just see the worst part of themselves in her
wikitext:
[]: Do Asuka haters just see the worst part of themselves in her?             Quote Post by C.T.1290 » Sun Feb 02, 2025 3:18 pm I’m taking about of course the people in the fandom who holds a great dislike towards Asuka Langley Soryu, and some people outside of it. Usually, these people would just refer to her as an annoying little bitch with a cargo full of issues with little to no redeeming qualities. Yes, I myself have been that person during my time on here, and I still feel a little that way sometimes. Although, something had occurred to me; do people hate her because she reminds them of their worse aspects that they’re trying to avoid? It’s just like that part in End of Evangelion; Asuka: Just the sight of you gets on my nerves! Shinji: Is it because I’m like you? And it’s not just Asuka, there are other people who reminds others of their own weaknesses, and that causes them to dislike one another. And it’s sort of why some bullying occurs. And it may be why Asuka would treat Shinji so harshly, because of her own self hatred and Shinji reminds her of that. And that could why many people in the fandom would have a great disdain towards her, aside from her obvious attitude.

[]: Re: Do Asuka haters just see the worst part of themselves in her?             Quote Post by gloomyAmateur » Mon Feb 03, 2025 7:05 am I think most of the people that hate her simply don't grasp that NONE of the characters in Evangelion are supposed to be likable, merely sympathetic and understandable.

[]: Re: Do Asuka haters just see the worst part of themselves in her?             Quote Post by viperzero » Tue Feb 04, 2025 7:46 am I think the idea “that people only dislike X character because it reminds them of themselves” is a dishonest one that no only denies people their opinion but also doesn’t allow true appreciation of the character. I think Asuka is really interesting and I have sympathy for her. However if anyone treated me the way she treats Shinji and the others they wouldn’t be part of my life. Similarly I think Travis Bickle is really interesting but I wouldn’t be hanging out with him. That’s not because I’m a vigilante in New York.

[]: Re: Do Asuka haters just see the worst part of themselves in her?             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Wed Feb 05, 2025 9:24 am gloomyAmateur wrote: I think most of the people that hate her simply don't grasp that NONE of the characters in Evangelion are supposed to be likable, merely sympathetic and understandable.

[]: Re: Do Asuka haters just see the worst part of themselves in her?             Quote Post by holy_roller » Sat Feb 08, 2025 1:55 am As an Asuka hater myself, I can't imagine myself being half as loud and annoying as Asuka is throughout the show. If anything I'm more like Shinji viperzero wrote: I think the idea “that people only dislike X character because it reminds them of themselves” is a dishonest one that no only denies people their opinion but also doesn’t allow true appreciation of the character.

[]: Re: Do Asuka haters just see the worst part of themselves in her?             Quote Post by viperzero » Sat Feb 08, 2025 10:09 am holy_roller wrote: As an Asuka hater myself, I can't imagine myself being half as loud and annoying as Asuka is throughout the show. If anything I'm more like Shinji viperzero#944464 wrote: I think the idea “that people only dislike X character because it reminds them of themselves” is a dishonest one that no only denies people their opinion but also doesn’t allow true appreciation of the character.

[]: Re: Do Asuka haters just see the worst part of themselves in her?             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Feb 16, 2025 5:01 pm I think it's more that Asuka is quite mean, and if you sympathize with the other characters it's easy to dislike her. The show has very lifelike dialogue, so she is a lot like an actual middle school bully. I always tended to be the weird kid (like Rei) so I had quite a lot of Asuka-like girls on my case. It's not silly or cartoonish in a way that you can dismiss. Though there's definitely also ppl that say shallow sexist stuff when you ask the reasons. (like saying she's "loud" and "a bitch" and wishing some disproportionate awfulness upon her. I've seen my share of terrible 'revenge fic'. Ppl offended by the idea of a girl who wouldn't be pliant to their wishes) Like I personally can separate my emotional response (would I be friends with her if she was real?) from my intellectual analysis (what does this character add to the story & themes?) or empathy (Do her actions make sense from her own Pov?) but not everyone does that, and if you don't, a character that seems like a jerk can't get sympathy or be a good character writing wise. A lot of classic literature has characters that are jerks. The "you see your own bad qualities in her" thing doesn't apply to her as much because while Shinji is in many ways unglamorous, Asuka is too "powerful" to have anyone's inner underdog projected onto. She's a prodigy, good looking, athletic, always putting others down & acting dominant... plenty of desirable stuff to balance out the flaws. It's easy to hate & quash sympathy for those you see as "powerful". (though school popularity matters little to the large scale plot where poor Asuka is just as much being used & exploited as the other pilots) A lot of the same people who hate Shinji identify with her because she seems dominant. Which is to some extent missing the point because her counterdependent tough guy attitude is explicitly her undoing & the cause of all her woes, but that doesn't mean that the people who like her don't legit recognize their own emotions in her.

[]: Re: Do Asuka haters just see the worst part of themselves in her?             Quote Post by Ringworm128 » Thu Mar 06, 2025 5:54 am She's a precious cinnamon muffin and she did nothing wrong. But in all seriousness Asuka's aggression is clearly put into context by her backstory and a tad over-exaggerated imo. Half of it is just childish banter between her and Shinji and she's shown to have moments where's civil or even friendly with other characters.

[]: Re: Do Asuka haters just see the worst part of themselves in her?             Quote Post by dotanuki » Thu Mar 06, 2025 11:07 am Asuka bullied Shinji all the time in the Anime because Shinji didn't have a courage to respond to Asuka's love. This made Asuka mad and made her bully Shinji as a response. As Asuka bullied Shinji, Shinji got even more affraid and withdrawn himself... Even made it less likely that Shinji respond her love. It was a vicious circle. I don't hate any of the Evangelion characters, but probably, Shinji is the character that gets the most hate because he is a covard and all the rest of the mecha main characters in the mecha mangas/animes that i read and watched: Gundam, Macross, Votoms, Laserion, Voltron, Dorvack, Red Baron, Gurren Lagann, Combattler V, Saber Rider, Knights of Sidonia were bad asses. lol So he drives an average mecha fan crazy. They are just not used to a covard as a main character. lol
